top_weaknesses.issue: Thesis ('models commoditize → UX/brand becomes moat') is correct but already consensus for HN/Lenny audience
top_weaknesses.fix: Add one contrarian wedge: defend why interface is MORE durable than distribution/data/procurement lock-in, or what kind of UX decision actually creates defensibility

top_weaknesses.issue: Perplexity/Harvey/Midjourney paragraph name-checks but doesn't prove — each supports a different kind of moat
top_weaknesses.fix: Pick one example and zoom in on the specific UX decision (Perplexity citation design, Harvey's narrowing, Midjourney's discord workflow) that created defensibility

top_weaknesses.issue: VC-strategy boilerplate ('owned surface', 'control plane', 'last mile between intelligence and action', 'compressed trust signal')
top_weaknesses.fix: Cut 3-4 abstract phrases; replace with plain operator language

top_weaknesses.issue: H2s are polished but generic enough to fit many essays — title doesn't surface the arena
top_weaknesses.fix: Tighten H1, e.g. 'When AI Capability Is Rented, Brand and UX Are the Company'; sharpen one H2 to name a mechanism

top_weaknesses.issue: Anchor text 'Agentforce' and the Menlo stat anchor are cryptic

consensus_summary: Quotable closer ('When the model is a commodity, the product is the company') and the ICP would share, but the argument is correct-and-consensus. One concrete UX-as-moat example and a contrarian wedge would unlock the next level.
